core_model.structure_summary: A scalar field evolves first-order in time as a second spatial derivative with a spatially varying coefficient, recast as pure diffusion plus a source term whose profile is fixed by a prescribed spatially varying coefficient, minus a state-dependent sink.
core_model.canonical_form: \partial_t u(x,t) = \partial_x\!\left[ a(x)\,\partial_x u(x,t) \right] - r(u) \;\; \Longleftrightarrow \;\; \partial_t u = D\,\partial_x^2 u + S(x)\,\partial_x u - r(u)

variables.meaning: scalar field defined along a one-dimensional continuum
arclength coordinate along a one-dimensional domain
spatially varying coefficient (cross-sectional measure and its derivatives) that sets the effective diffusivity and the geometric source term

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A scalar field on a one-dimensional continuum obeys a first-order-in-time linear diffusion equation with a spatially varying diffusivity, equivalently pure diffusion plus a space-dependent source/transport term.

notes: Central result is the generalized parabolic evolution equation with a geometry-induced variable coefficient, which the authors explicitly rewrite as a diffusion equation with a source term set by the cross-sectional-area profile and its derivatives. The state-dependent sink (ionic-current analog) makes the full model mixed-linearity; stripping it leaves a linear variable-coefficient diffusion PDE. Frenet-Serret / first-fundamental-form geometry is auxiliary machinery used to derive the variable coefficient, not the core dynamical object.
